meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Russia holds downsized Victory Day parade
items.summary: Putin is set to speak at the World War II commemoration, which is scaled back this year due to security concerns.

items.title: Spain’s Canary Islands brace for incoming hantavirus-stricken cruise ship
items.summary: 140 passengers and crew on the MV Hondius vessel will be 'completely isolated' and evacuated, say Spanish authorities.

items.title: Israeli settlers set fire to homes and cars in violent West Bank raids
items.summary: Dozens of Israeli settlers stormed various areas of the West Bank, set cars on fire, and attacked Palestinians.

items.title: Financial struggles burden Yemen’s army as soldiers wait for wages
items.summary: Yemeni soldiers earn $38 to $116 monthly, but an unstable currency has eroded the value of their salaries.

items.title: Wembanyama powers Spurs past T-Wolves as Knicks beat Sixers in NBA playoffs
items.summary: San Antonio take 2-1 semifinal series lead over Minnesota, while New York Knicks are 3-0 up against Philadelphia 76ers.

items.title: Cambodians struggle with displaced lives amid tense ceasefire with Thailand
items.summary: Families displaced by fighting fear new clashes with Thailand, say education among areas most affected by border war.

items.title: Iran war live: US expects Tehran’s reply to peace deal; ‘clashes’ in Hormuz
items.summary: Israel killed 31 people in southern Lebanon on Friday, including a rescue worker, Lebanon's National News Agency said.

items.title: Pentagon releases video of strikes on Iranian oil tankers
items.summary: Footage released by the Pentagon shows US strikes on two Iranian oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Celebrated naturalist David Attenborough marks 100th birthday
items.summary: Britain’s famed natural historian David Attenborough is celebrating his 100th birthday.

items.title: Costa Rica inaugurates right-wing president Laura Fernandez
items.summary: Costa Rica inaugurated right-wing president Laura Fernandez on Friday as supporters gathered in the national stadium.

items.title: Laura Fernandez sworn in as Costa Rica’s new president
items.summary: Fernandez enters office with her right-wing party holding an absolute majority in the legislature.

items.title: Venice Biennale: The Art of the Unseen
items.summary: At this year’s Venice Biennale, the story isn’t just about what’s on display—but what’s missing.

items.title: Could Labour and Conservative party dominance in UK politics be ending?
items.summary: Britain's prime minister is under pressure to quit after huge losses in local elections.

items.title: Al Jazeera reporter witnesses Israeli strikes on Lebanon
items.summary: Israel has increased its attacks across southern Lebanon. Al Jazeera’s Obaida Hitto reports from Tyre.

items.title: Protests in the Canary Islands as virus-stricken ship heads for port
items.summary: Protesters in Tenerife are voicing opposition to the imminent arrival of a cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak

items.title: Libya’s largest oil refinery halts operations during fighting
items.summary: Zawiya refinery shut down in 'precautionary measure' as emergency declared following explosions and gunfire nearby.

items.title: Four convicted in US related to killing of Haitian President Jovenel Moise
items.summary: Prosecutors charge Florida served as central hub in 2021 assassination of Moise, which set off ongoing political crisis.

items.title: Trump announces three-day ceasefire in Russia-Ukraine war
items.summary: Truce will also include swap of 1,000 prisoners of war from each country, US president says.

items.title: ASEAN leaders adopt measures to ease economic pain caused by Iran war
items.summary: The bloc currently imports more than half of its crude oil from the Middle East.

items.title: Olympic water polo resignations cite lack of respect, belittling behaviour
items.summary: Olympic and European holders in mass resignation, saying Serbia chief has shown a lack of respect for their efforts.

items.title: How and when can Arsenal or Man City win the Premier League?
items.summary: Manchester City face Brentford on Saturday, while Arsenal travel to West Ham on Sunday in red-hot Premier League race.

items.title: More than a dozen reported killed in Israeli attacks on south Lebanon
items.summary: Israeli air raids on southern Lebanon escalate ahead of new round of talks expected next week in Washington, DC.

items.title: Justice for Shireen: The American investigation
items.summary: Four years after Shireen Abu Akleh’s killing, her family is still seeking US-backed accountability.

items.title: Real Madrid fine Valverde and Tchouameni for dressing-room fight
items.summary: Real do not impose sporting sanctions saying 500,000 euro fines concludes the 'internal procedures' against the pair.

items.title: South Koreans gear up to roar on football team from rival North
items.summary: Naegohyang Women's FC will play the Suwon FC Women on May 20 in the Asian Champions League in a six-year first.
